english: Japanese Mackerel Prawn Tempura Bento Box
japanese: サバと海老天ぷら弁当

food_subgroup: Mixed seafood bento with tempura and grilled fish over rice

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general bento food product class. No specific saba-and-prawn-tempura bento entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated bento code. A00LC (composite fish and seafood dishes) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific Japanese bento combination.

main_ingredients: tempura batter (wheat flour, egg, cold water)
vegetable sides (typically pickled vegetables, edamame, or salad)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Japanese bento box containing grilled saba fillet, two to three pieces of prawn tempura, and a portion of steamed white rice with vegetable sides, weighing approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Atlantic Mackerel (grilled), Shrimp (breaded and fried), and Cooked White Rice (short-grain), weighted by typical bento composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for saba-and-prawn-tempura bento as a composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component entries: Atlantic mackerel cooked (FDC 175127), shrimp breaded and fried (FDC 175180), and cooked short-grain white rice (FDC 169756), combined in a representative bento weight ratio of approximately 25% mackerel, 20% prawn tempura, 40% rice, and 15% vegetable sides. Per-100g values are the weighted average of these components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; white rice (short-grain, cooked) GI reference used as dominant carbohydrate component
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for saba-and-prawn-tempura bento as a composite dish. The GI is estimated at 54 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being short-grain white rice (GI ~54–56 per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables). Tempura batter and fish protein components lower the effective GI relative to plain rice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 83.25) / 100 ≈ 44.9, rounded to 44.8; carbohydrate per serving = 18.5 g/100g × 450 g = 83.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full bento serving (~450 g) delivers approximately 1395 mg sodium, representing 58–70% of the recommended daily limit of 2000–2400 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a bento box or request reduced-sodium soy sauce and seasoning, as the sodium load from soy-based marinades and tempura dipping sauce can approach or exceed the recommended single-meal sodium ceiling of 600–800 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~44.8) driven by a substantial portion of short-grain white rice, which has a low GI (~54) but contributes a large carbohydrate mass per bento serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consider reducing the rice portion to approximately 100–150 g and increasing the proportion of fish and vegetable sides to lower the overall glycemic load per meal; pairing with vinegared rice or brown rice would further attenuate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saba (mackerel) is a rich source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are associated with reduced triglycerides and anti-inflammatory cardiovascular benefit; saturated fat content is moderate at approximately 1.4 g per 100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The omega-3 fatty acid content from grilled saba provides cardiovascular benefit; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the deep-fried prawn tempura component, which adds saturated fat and dietary cholesterol, and should prefer bento preparations with grilled rather than fried protein where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Saba and Prawn Tempura Bento is a Japanese-style bento box meal combining two distinct seafood preparations: a grilled fillet of saba (mackerel), typically seasoned with salt or a light soy-mirin glaze, and one to three pieces of prawn (ebi) tempura, consisting of whole prawns coated in a light, airy wheat-flour-and-egg batter and deep-fried until golden and crisp. The bento is served alongside a portion of steamed Japanese short-grain white rice and typically includes small vegetable accompaniments such as pickled daikon, edamame, or a simple salad. Saba has a firm, oily flesh with a pronounced savoury and slightly fishy flavour, while the prawn tempura provides a contrasting light crunch and delicate sweetness. The dish is a staple of Japanese bento culture and is widely available in Singapore through Japanese fast-casual restaurant chains. Nutritionally, the dish is notable for its high-quality marine protein, significant omega-3 fatty acid content from the mackerel, and moderate energy density. The rice component contributes the majority of carbohydrates and drives a medium glycemic load per serving. Sodium content is elevated due to seasoning and soy-based condiments. The dish is not suitable for individuals with fish, crustacean, gluten, or egg allergies. Clinically, the omega-3 content supports cardiovascular health, but the sodium load and glycemic load require monitoring for individuals with hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes.
common_user_queries: What is in a saba bento?
Calories in saba prawn tempura bento
Japanese bento with grilled fish and fried shrimp

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite bento dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component entries for grilled Atlantic mackerel, breaded and fried shrimp, and cooked short-grain white rice, combined in a representative weight ratio. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (short-grain white rice) using the Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy component entries; no single approved-source record exists for this exact bento combination. 2. Serving size of 450 g is a representative estimate for a standard Japanese bento box as sold in Singapore fast-casual restaurants; actual restaurant portion may vary by 50–80 g. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the component ingredients under standard preparation. 4. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on short-grain white rice as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate; the actual GI of the composite dish has not been directly measured. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.1), polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6), potassium_mg (220.0), calcium_mg (28.0), iron_mg (0.8), cholesterol_mg (62.0), vitamin_c_mg (1.2), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (18.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.08), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.12), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (3.4), vitamin_b6_mg (0.22), vitamin_b12_mcg (3.8), folate_mcg_dfe (12.0), vitamin_d_mcg (2.1), vitamin_e_mg (0.9), phosphorus_mg (160.0), magnesium_mg (28.0), zinc_mg (0.8), selenium_mcg (22.0), and water_g (62.0) are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are not individually confirmed by a single-dish approved-source entry. 6. Halal status is marked false as saba mackerel is a scaled fish (permissible) but the prawn tempura batter and preparation environment at the specific restaurant have not been independently verified as halal-certified. 7. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_00002809 is used as the closest parent bento class; no exact FoodOn entry for this specific dish exists. 8. FoodEx2 code A00LC is used as the closest parent composite fish and seafood dish category.
